extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam sprinted to his second stage win after positioning conservatively for 5km, beating DÉMARE Arnaud who was caught out by late-race crashes.
tactical_patterns: GEOGHEGAN HART Tao worked early to control pace for Sky's GC duo, suggesting team willingness to burn domestiques defending yellow.
TRENTIN Matteo's Mitchelton-Scott team paced final categorized climb despite no GC interest, positioning sprinter for bonus seconds not stage win.
KWIATKOWSKI Michał contested intermediate sprint bonus seconds at 4km despite holding yellow, revealing Sky's concern over 18-second gap to BERNAL Egan.
Late crashes on technical descent split peloton but front group neutralized for regrouping, indicating unwritten truce before queen stage.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Conserved positioning energy in final 5km relying on team rather than fighting early, tactical maturity suggesting Milan-Sanremo readiness
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: Second place despite being set up suggests final kick blunted, worrying pattern after losing to BENNETT on stage three
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: negative: Dropped on category-2 climb while wearing polka dots reveals jersey won through breakaways not climbing strength
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Dropped before final climb on moderate stage suggests poor form, ominous for upcoming sprint opportunities
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: Contesting intermediate sprint bonuses with only 18-second GC lead shows defensive mindset, vulnerable position acknowledged
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Protected in top-2 overall position with GEOGHEGAN HART working suggests Sky preparing dual-leader strategy for queen stage
